title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
summary: RL Nabors argues that small local models can handle most inference workloads more cost-effectively than frontier models, and provides a framework for deciding when to replace frontier calls with smaller models. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary and methods for measuring model performance on production traces to make data-driven model selection decisions.
writeup: RL Nabors of Arize argues that small local models can handle most inference workloads more cost-effectively than frontier models, and presents a framework for deciding when to swap a frontier call for a smaller one. The talk grounds that decision in evaluation: it covers evaluation vocabulary and methods for measuring model performance on production traces, so model selection becomes data-driven rather than assumed. Related concepts include golden datasets, test-time compute, and model compression. The takeaway is that measuring performance on real traces can justify replacing frontier calls with on-device models for many tasks.
